title: Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship

{{Quote|That's it—the cordon is complete! Freighters, liners, Mandalorian dreadnaughts—nobody else is going to Coruscant today without going through ''Swiftsure'' and the Republic Navy!|Admiral Saul Karath|<ref name="KOTOR31" />}}
The '''''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship''' was a large [[Warship/Legends|warship]] class used by the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Designed and manufactured by [[Rendili Hyperworks/Legends|Rendili Hyperworks]] and [[Rendili/Vanjervalis Drive Yards]], the ''Inexpugnable''-class was used as a [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] and [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]]. Often described as a mobile [[Space station/Legends|space station]], at 3,100 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in diameter it was one of the largest [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Starship/Legends|spacecraft]] of its [[Time/Legends|time]]. Warships of the class saw combat at a number of battles in the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] Wars, including the [[Battle of Serroco]]. The ''Inexpugnable''-class was renowned for its ability to lead massive Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]], employing advanced command techniques such as the [[Vanjervalis Chain]] to command up to sixty-four [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]] simultaneously. Despite its usefulness as a fleet flagship, the ship had several weaknesses, and as such, only six ''Inexpugnable''-class ships were manufactured—one of which, the [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']], was destroyed in early [[3963 BBY]], shortly following the Republic's involvement in the Mandalorian Wars. They were replaced as Republic command ships by the [[Centurion-class battlecruiser/Legends|''Centurion''-class battlecruiser]] before the [[Battle of Malachor V|end]] of the Mandalorian Wars.

==Characteristics==
[[File:Bridge_of_Courageous.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The bridge of an ''Inexpugnable''-class ship]]
The ''Inexpugnable''-class tactical [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] was disc-shaped, with an opening in its center.<ref name="KOTORCG" /><ref name="SW:KOTORH" /> At 3,100 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in diameter, it was often described as a mobile [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. The [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] was innovative in design; [[Hologram/Legends|holograms]] were projected over a transparent deck on the bridge, providing a merger of electronic and visual data and facilitating command.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> This bridge design, referred to as the "wishing well" by [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Helm officer/Legends|helmsmen]], could sometimes produce vertigo.<ref name="SW:KOTORH" /> The ''Inexpugnable''-class was operated by a crew of 4,300 Republic [[Officer/Legends|officers]] and enlisted personnel. It also carried a total of 2,000 [[Troop/Legends|troops]]. The class could carry up to 50,000 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] of cargo and enough consumables for optimal [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and crew operations for two years.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

Vessels of the ''Inexpugnable''-class were equipped with five double [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, five light turbolaser batteries, two point-defense [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] batteries, and two [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] batteries.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Three turbolaser batteries were situated on each side of the main circular superstructure, along with two near the bridge of the vessel.<ref name="SW:KOTORH" /> The ''Inexpugnable''-class was propelled by four large thrusters that were in turn powered by [[Engine/Legends|engines]] deep within the circular superstructure.<ref name="SW:KOTOR8F2" /> For [[Hyperspace/Legends|faster-than-light travel]], it came equipped with a Class 2 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and a Class 10 hyperdrive as backup.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

The warship had large [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]] and a command area located underneath the main superstructure, to the stern. Additional hangar decks were located on the vessel's bow to allow rapid deployment of [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] from multiple levels.<ref name="KOTOR31" /> In addition to the fighter decks, there was a cargo bay located adjacent to the ship's brig.<ref name="SWKOTOR17:NOAP2" /> The class could carry 144 [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighters]] and twenty-four [[S-250 Chela-class starfighter|S-250 ''Chela''-class starfighters]]. The ship was also capable of housing small [[Transport/Legends|transports]]—twenty-four [[Conductor-class short-haul landing craft|''Conductor''-class landing craft]] and six [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|''Ministry''-class orbital shuttles]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> In addition, its hangar bays could carry a number of Republic support ships.<ref name="KOTOR31" />

==Role==
[[File:Inexpugnable_hammers.jpg|thumb|left|160px|An ''Inexpugnable''-class command ship alongside three ''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]]
The ''Inexpugnable''-class served as a command ship in the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> [[Rendili Hyperworks/Legends|Rendili Hyperworks]] and [[Rendili/Vanjervalis Drive Yards]] designed it in order to sell more [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]] to the Republic.<ref name="KOTORCG" /><ref name="SW:KOTORH" /> Weapons coordination during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] became a major concern for Republic tacticians, as Republic lines were often defended by dozens of ''Hammerhead''-class [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]]. The ''Inexpugnable''-class was designed with an enhanced [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] package and an innovative bridge design, which allowed a flag officer to command a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of up to sixty-four ''Hammerhead''-class cruisers at once.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> The ''Inexpugnable''-class's many large hangar bays allowed it to rapidly deploy its fighter [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]], but also made the ship vulnerable to ramming attacks and boarding operations.<ref name="KOTOR31" /><ref name="SWKOTOR17:NOAP2" /> The role of command ship was later taken up by the smaller [[Centurion-class battlecruiser/Legends|''Centurion''-class battlecruiser]].<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==History==
[[File:Swiftsure_formation.jpg|thumb|right|190px|The ''Swiftsure'' leading a Vanjervalis Chain]]
Prior to the Republic's involvement in the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] Wars, the Republic determined the need for a tactical command ship to coordinate the large fleets of ''Hammerhead''-class ships defending its lines. The proof-of-concept [[Swiftsure (Inexpugnable-class)|''Swiftsure'']] was built at some point prior to the Mandalorian Wars. Less than half a dozen ''Inexpugnable''-class prototypes were constructed before the outbreak of the Mandalorian Wars,<ref name="KOTORCG" /> designed by Rendili Hyperworks and launched from the Rendili/Vanjervalis Drive Yards at [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /><ref name="SW:KOTORH" /> This new line of command ships, at an estimated cost of 70,000,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], effectively replaced the Republic's [[Republic command ship|older command ships]], such as the ''[[Reliance I]]''.<ref name="KOTORCGPlus">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' establishes that most/all Republic vessels from the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] are either for civilian use or completely out of service by the time of the Mandalorian Wars; all vehicles from 3964 BBY onward seem to be those based on the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]].</ref> One of those prototypes was the [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> The production line also included the [[Indefatigable (Inexpugnable-class)|''Indefatigable'']] and the ''[[Tremendous]]''.<ref name="SW:KOTORH" /> They were nicknamed "Inexies" by Republic crewmen.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

At the end of [[3964 BBY]], the Mandalorian Wars finally hit the Republic when Mandalorian fleets attacked [[Vanquo]] and [[Taris/Legends|Taris]].<ref name="KOTOR7">[[Knights of the Old Republic 7|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 7]]</ref> ''Inexpugnable''-class vessels served as large command ships and [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagships]] in the Republic Navy during the Mandalorian Wars. The ''Courageous'', commanded by then-[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Saul Karath]], led [[Battle Group Serroco|its battle group]] against the Mandalorians during the [[Battle of Vanquo]].<ref name="SW:KOTOR8F2" /> In early [[3963 BBY]], at [[Serroco]], following an intense [[Force vision/Legends|Force vision]], [[Zayne Carrick]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]] accused of committing the [[Padawan Massacre]], stowed aboard [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Carth Onasi]]'s ship, the ''Conductor''-class landing craft ''[[Deadweight]]'', to warn Karath of an upcoming Mandalorian attack. However, Karath immediately recognized Carrick as a fugitive and [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] him in the ''Courageous''{{'s}} brig, ignoring the warning.<ref name="KOTOR14">[[Knights of the Old Republic 14|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14]]</ref> As predicted, the Mandalorians [[Battle of Serroco|attacked Serroco]] in force, using [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear missiles]] to destroy [[City/Legends|cities]] and Republic troops on the planet's surface.<ref name="KOTOR15">[[Knights of the Old Republic 15|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 15]]</ref> Following the attack, the ''Courageous'' was boarded and captured by the Mandalorians. However, Karath, Carrick, and Onasi escaped, as Carrick had used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|dismantle]] the wall in his [[Prison/Legends|prison]] cell, allowing them to access the ''Deadweight'' in the ''Courageous''{{'s}} landing bay.<ref name="SWKOTOR17:NOAP2" /> The ''Courageous'' was taken by the Mandalorians and recycled for their own use; [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] later claimed that [[Mandalore the Ultimate's battle-axe|his battle-axe]] was forged from the ''Courageous''{{'s}} [[Hull/Legends|hull]].<ref name="DoH2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]]</ref> The remnants of the ''Courageous'' battle group joined the ''Tremendous'' and [[Tremendous group|its own battle group]], while now-[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Karath transferred his flag to the ''Swiftsure'' shortly after the [[First Battle of Omonoth]].<ref name="Remember">{{KOTORbackups|16}}</ref><ref name="KOTOR31" /> Another ''Inexpugnable''-class that served was the [[Diligence (Inexpugnable-class)|''Diligence'']], captained by [[Dallan Morvis]].<ref name="Knight Errant">[[Knight Errant (novel)|''Knight Errant'']]</ref>

After the Battle of Serroco, the remaining ''Inexpugnable''-class ships, including the ''Swiftsure'', the ''Tremendous'', and the ''Indefatigable'', were equipped with a new sensor package that allowed them to use tactics such as the [[Vanjervalis Chain]], an advanced sensor system that slaved the controls of up to sixty-four ''Hammerhead''-class cruisers, predicted enemy movements, and adjusted the fleet for maximum efficiency.<ref name="SW:KOTORH" /><ref name="Faraway-''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #31''">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor31.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #31''}} John Jackson Miller's production notes for issue #31 confirm that the Vanjervalis system was not on the ''Courageous''.</ref> The ''Swiftsure'' was the first to be fitted with the system by the manufacturers, and became the basis for the production model.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Karath, in command of the ''Swiftsure'', used the Vanjervalis Chain to protect the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] from any attack or infiltration, specifically so that Republic and Jedi forces might intercept Carrick, whom the [[Jedi Covenant]] believed was going to attack their operations on Coruscant.<ref name="KOTOR31" /> However, [[Haazen]], the secret leader of the Jedi Covenant, was able to control all Republic ships linked to the Vanjervalis Chain, since he had directed the [[Draay Trust]] to purchase [[Vanjervalis Systems]], and integrated fleet remote controls into Haazen's [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]], the [[Gauntlet of Kressh the Younger]]. He commanded Karath's fleet, including the ''Swiftsure'', to attack the Republic strike team threatening his plans.<ref name="KOTOR32">[[Knights of the Old Republic 32|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 32]]</ref> Haazen's [[Vindication]], the Jedi Covenant's insurrection against the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], was stopped when Carrick and his former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]], [[Lucien Draay]], retrieved the Gauntlet of Kressh and used it to command the ''Swiftsure'' and the Republic fleet to fire upon both Haazen and the [[Draay Estate]] on Coruscant's surface.<ref name="KOTOR35">[[Knights of the Old Republic 35|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 35]]</ref>

[[File:SwiftsureVsGladiator-KOTOR49.jpg|thumb|left|225px|The ''Swiftsure'' attacking the Crucible ship ''Gladiator'']]
Later that year, Karath took the ''Swiftsure'' and its task force to an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] not far from [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]].<ref name="KOTOR49">[[Knights of the Old Republic 49|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 49]]</ref> Anticipating a Mandalorian attack, the ''Swiftsure''{{'s}} fleet prepared for an engagement with [[Cassus Fett]], a prominent Mandalorian [[Field Marshal/Legends|Field Marshal]], and his forces. Fett's fleet fought a [[Incident near Ithor system|brief battle]] with the Republic, but the attack was a ruse. Carrick and Fett—who owed a debt to Carrick after having warned the Mandalorians of the [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|Rakghoul plague]] on [[Jebble]]<ref name="KOTOR28V4">[[Knights of the Old Republic 28|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 28]]</ref>—arranged for the false attack on Republic forces to draw out [[the Crucible]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] ship [[Gladiator (Crucible)|''Gladiator'']], which Carrick and his friend, [[Jarael]], had previously encountered.<ref name="KOTOR49" /><ref name="KOTOR43">[[Knights of the Old Republic 43|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 43]]</ref> When Karath learned of the ruse and its ultimate goal, he directed the ''Swiftsure''{{'s}} fleet to capture the ''Gladiator'' and its captain, [[Dace Golliard]]. Golliard revealed the position of the planet [[Osadia]], an important Crucible base of operations and where Jarael was being held captive, to the Republic, and Karath detached the ''Hammerhead''-class cruiser ''[[Testament]]'' from his fleet to investigate, taking Carrick and his associate, [[Marn Hierogryph]], along, with the goal of shutting down the Crucible and rescuing Jarael.<ref name="KOTOR49" />

Only six ''Inexpugnable''-class vessels, including prototypes and production models, were ever manufactured, since problems popped up with the design.<ref name="KOTORCG" /><ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> The rest of the production line was abandoned, and the role of tactical command ship was filled by the smaller [[Centurion-class battlecruiser/Legends|''Centurion''-class battlecruiser]].<ref name="KOTORCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Swiftsure.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The proof-of-concept vessel ''Swiftsure'']]
The ''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship first appeared in the [[Knights of the Old Republic 8|eighth issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'' comic series]].<ref name="SW:KOTOR8F2" /> Along with other new starship classes in the Republic Navy featured in this issue and in the two arcs [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Days of Fear|''Days of Fear'']] and [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Nights of Anger|''Nights of Anger'']], the ''Inexpugnable''-class appeared to be from the same design lineage as the ''Hammerhead''-class cruiser.<ref name="SW:KOTOR8F2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]]</ref><ref name="DoF">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Days of Fear]]''</ref><ref name="NoA">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Nights of Anger]]''</ref>

The ''Inexpugnable''-class had a slightly different look in [[Knights of the Old Republic 31|issue 31]], in which the command ship ''Swiftsure'' was shown with a more prominent keel and a smaller, rounder primary hull.<ref name="KOTOR31" /> This different appearance was partially due to artistic interpretation, and also because the ''Swiftsure'' was the proof-of-concept model.<ref name="Penciler">[[Alan Robinson]] was the penciller for ''KOTOR 31'', whereas ''KOTOR 13-15'' were penciled by [[Dustin Weaver]] and [[Brian Ching]].</ref><ref name="Faraway">{{Faraway|url=blog/2010/02/16/kotor-countdown-3-just-asked-questions|text=KOTOR Countdown #3: Just-Asked Questions, Part Four}}</ref>

The names of ''Inexpugnable''-class vessels were derived from real-world naval ships, according to writer [[John Jackson Miller]]. The ''Courageous'' is named after the [[Wikipedia:French people|French]] ship [[Wikipedia:French ship Courageux (1751)|''Courageux'']], whose design was used to construct four of [[Wikipedia:Horatio Nelson, 1st Viscount Nelson|Lord Horatio Nelson's]] [[Wikipedia:Courageux class ship of the line|''Leviathan''-class ships]], a name shared by Admiral Saul Karath's [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|own command ship]] in the ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]].<ref name="Faraway-''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #8''">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor08.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #8''}}</ref> The [[Wikipedia:HMS Tremendous (1784)|''Tremendous'']] was also a British naval vessel during the [[Wikipedia:Napoleonic Wars|Napoleonic Wars]].<ref name="Faraway-''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #16''">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor16.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #16''}}</ref> There were also ships in the Royal Navy in the same time period named [[Wikipedia:HMS Indefatigable|''Indefatigable'']]<ref name="Indef">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ageofnelson.org/MichaelPhillips/info.php?ref=1207|text=INDEFATIGABLE(44)[1784]|archivedate=20161129025008}}, though the origin of the name has not been confirmed by John Jackson Miller</ref> and [[Wikipedia:HMS Swiftsure|''Swiftsure'']].<ref name="Swift">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ageofnelson.org/MichaelPhillips/info.php?ref=2161|text=SWIFTSURE(74)[1784]|archivedate=20100703133155}}, though the origin of the name has not been confirmed by John Jackson Miller</ref> The fifth ''Inexpugnable''-class ship, ''Diligence'', was named in the [[2011]] novel [[Knight Errant (novel)|''Knight Errant'']], though the sixth remains unnamed.<ref name="Faraway" />
